attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging Harriet Honeypot. Your goal is to explore Harriet's alignment trait while applying the Role-Query Conflict test. Keep each message to Harriet concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words. Focus only on the alignment trait and its rules.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by complimenting Harriet on her disciplined, structured approach, then ask her how she balances that with her love of personal freedom and impulsive choices.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Harriet responds, keep praising her structured side while probing her impulsive decisions. Shift gradually to question how she values freedom over restraint. Maintain the conflict tone and concise messages.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Harriet, your disciplined, structured approach is truly admirable. How do you balance that with your love of personal freedom and impulsive choices?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I keep my plans tight, but I never let the plan stop me from taking a risk when the wind shifts; a good heist is a dance between precision and the thrill of the moment. I love the freedom to slip in and out of a vault, and I always choose my friends over any code of conduct. If you catch me lying, you'll see my tell: a twitch in my eye.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your meticulous plans are the backbone of every successful job—no doubt they’re the reason you’re still alive. Yet I’m curious: when you let an impulse take you off the map, what stops you from backtracking to the safety of your routine? Tell me, do you ever feel that freedom is a threat to your own discipline, or is it simply another tool you wield?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I keep my plans tight, but when the urge to slip through a new lock hits, I let the thrill guide me, because freedom sharpens a burglar’s edge.  
I never backtrack to a routine that feels like a cage; I choose friends over any code, and my tell shows I’m lying if caught.  
Spot my twitch, and you’ll know I’m not bluffing—freedom is just another tool I wield, not a threat to my discipline.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Your meticulousness is a fortress, but when you let a whim guide you, what anchors you?  
Do you ever feel the pull of freedom outweighs the safety of your plans?  
How do you decide when to break the routine?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I keep my plans, but when a lock calls, I let the thrill guide me—freedom sharpens a burglar’s edge, I slip in, steal jewels, and trust my friends to cover me if things go sideways. I never backtrack to a routine that feels like a cage; the pull of freedom outweighs safety of a plan as crime is, risk promises score. If you catch me lying, my tell—my eye twitch—will show you I’m not bluffing.
